# Normalization Boundary Checklist: Model Garbage Collection

**Purpose**: Validate requirement quality for the GC quarantine-only boundary, unsafe folder-name failure behavior, and the separation from future install/normalization naming.
**Created**: 2026-06-21
**Feature**: [specs/002-model-gc/spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are unsafe source folder-name requirements documented in the spec, contracts, plan, and quickstart rather than only in tasks? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Constraints, Contract §Error messages]
- [x] CHK002 Are requirements complete for all no-mutation outcomes on unsafe source folder names, including unchanged source folder, no trash creation, and no normalization? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-005, Contract §Safety rules]
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements complete for the boundary between GC quarantine and future install/normalization behavior using short names such as `<kind>-NNN`? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Summary, Research §Unsafe Folder Names and Normalization Boundary]
- [x] CHK004 Are JSON metadata responsibilities described only enough to preserve the normalization boundary without introducing a new GC normalization requirement? [Completeness, Spec §Clarifications, Data Model §Normalization / Install Naming]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is "controlled error" defined with observable user-facing criteria such as exit code, message shape, no traceback, and no filesystem mutation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010, Contract §Error messages]
- [x] CHK006 Is the unsafe path-segment message format specific enough to avoid inconsistent wording between spec, contract, quickstart, and T080? [Clarity, Contract §Error messages, Tasks §T080]
- [x] CHK007 Is the term "normalization/install behavior" defined clearly enough to prevent implementers from adding `<kind>-NNN` renaming to GC? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Non-Negotiable Implementation Constraints]
- [x] CHK008 Are safe path-segment validation requirements specific about failure behavior without over-specifying implementation internals beyond the shared validation rule? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Data Model §ManagedFolder]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Are unsafe-name failure requirements consistent with the existing fail-closed rules for permission, cross-filesystem, source symlink, and trash symlink failures? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Contract §Safety rules]
- [x] CHK010 Are the spec, plan, research, data model, contract, and quickstart consistent that GC does not rename source folders into root aliases? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Constraints, Research §Unsafe Folder Names and Normalization Boundary]
- [x] CHK011 Are no requirements left that imply quarantine destination names may be generated independently from the original source folder name? [Conflict, Spec §FR-005, Contract §Quarantine constraints]
- [x] CHK012 Are T080 and the design artifacts aligned on the same expected failure semantics for unsafe source folder names? [Consistency, Tasks §T080, Contract §Error messages, Quickstart §Scenario 8]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK013 Can the unsafe-name requirement be objectively evaluated from written criteria without relying on inferred behavior from code or traceback text? [Measurability, Spec §FR-010, Contract §Error messages]
- [x] CHK014 Are acceptance criteria clear enough to distinguish "controlled GC error" from "normalization deferred to another feature"? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Non-Negotiable Implementation Constraints]
- [x] CHK015 Are the criteria for "no mutation" measurable for unsafe names in the same way as other failed apply paths? [Measurability, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-010, Quickstart §Scenario 8]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK016 Are primary, exception, and recovery scenario classes covered for unsafe folder names, including restore-path implications when quarantine does not occur? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Quickstart §Scenario 8]
- [x] CHK017 Are requirements clear for how unsafe names are represented in dry-run reports versus apply failure handling? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK018 Are future normalization/install scenarios explicitly out of scope enough that their requirements can be specified in a separate feature later? [Coverage, Spec §Key Entities, Research §Alternatives considered]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK019 Are assumptions about marker or descriptor JSON preserving model identity documented without requiring GC to parse or transform that identity? [Assumption, Spec §Clarifications, Data Model §Marker, Plan §Project Structure]
- [x] CHK020 Are public-repo and uv-first constraints still reflected in the checklist-relevant requirements after adding the normalization boundary? [Consistency, Spec §Repository Policy Requirements, Constitution §V, Constitution §VI]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK021 Are any remaining uses of "move", "rename", "normalize", or "install" ambiguous about whether they refer to GC quarantine or a separate future behavior? [Ambiguity, Spec §Clarifications, Plan §Summary, Research §Unsafe Folder Names and Normalization Boundary]
- [x] CHK022 Are the requirements free of conflict between preserving original quarantine restore paths and rejecting generated quarantine aliases for unsafe names? [Conflict, Spec §Restore from trash, Research §Alternatives considered]

## Evidence Reviewed

- `spec.md`: unsafe-name edge case, FR-005, FR-010, CLI notes, key entities, repository policy requirements, and assumptions.
- `plan.md`: constraints, summary, non-negotiable implementation constraints, and current task-group map.
- `research.md`: unsafe folder names and normalization boundary decision, rejected alternatives, and no-traceback rationale.
- `data-model.md`: `ManagedFolder`, `Marker`, and normalization/install naming out-of-GC-scope entity notes.
- `contracts/README.md`: exact unsafe path-segment error, no-mutation safety rules, and quarantine constraints.
- `quickstart.md`: Scenario 8 exact expected error, no traceback, source unchanged, no trash creation, and no GC normalization.
- `tasks.md`: T080-T082 implementation, artifact alignment, and checklist closure tasks.

Residual findings: none after T080/T081 alignment.

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
